analyze-project.summary: Creates persistent analysis of existing code, papers, or documents; initial analysis defaults here unless read-only/no-file or another primary applies.

invocation.use_when: Use when the user asks to analyze existing code, a paper, or a document and no usable persistent analysis exists, or to refresh stale analysis; default initial analysis to persistent output.
invocation.not_for: Not for conversational, read-only, or no-file analysis; orientation, context recovery, status, experiments, external research, source changes, or completed-work audits.

analyze-user.summary: Create or update a cross-project user-preference profile from coding, writing, and analysis patterns.

invocation.use_when: Use when durable cross-project user preferences must be inferred from coding, writing, or analysis evidence and stored as a profile.
invocation.not_for: Not for one-project context recovery, casual preference acknowledgment, or ordinary task execution.

audit.summary: Read-oriented post-run inspection for artifact drift, inconsistency, and omissions.

invocation.use_when: Use when completed work or artifacts need a read-oriented inspection for drift, inconsistency, omissions, or unsupported claims.
invocation.not_for: Not for implementing fixes, producing the primary artifact, or replacing execution-stage verification.

autopilot-apply.summary: Apply a cheatsheet draft to the real source artifact and verify the result.

invocation.use_when: Use when an approved cheatsheet or patching guide must be applied to the real source artifact and the applied result verified.
invocation.not_for: Not for drafting or refining the cheatsheet, or for unrelated source implementation.

autopilot-code.summary: Code-work entrypoint that detects spec context and closes the plan→execute→test→report loop.

invocation.use_when: Use when source code must be implemented, debugged, refactored, or code-audited through a plan, execution, test, and report loop.
invocation.not_for: Not for requirements-only changes, new empirical experiments, or document-only editing.

invocation.use_when: Use when a visual product surface needs references, design tokens, components or mockups, review, and development handoff.
invocation.not_for: Not for implementing an already-approved design in code or for document prose work.

autopilot-design.argument_shape: <design task or app path> [--scope ui|webapp|slide|icon|diagram|mixed] [--artifact standalone|project] [--from <phase>] [--intensity direct|quick|standard|strong|thorough|adversarial]

autopilot-draft.summary: Document-drafting pipeline that produces an applicable artifact through strategy, drafting, verification, and editing.

invocation.use_when: Use when a new paper, presentation, report, proposal, or other user-facing document must be produced from evidence.
invocation.not_for: Not for correcting only an existing document or for source-code implementation.

autopilot-lab.summary: Rapid experiment prototyping around training setup and checkpoint evaluation/analysis.

invocation.use_when: Use when training setup, checkpoint evaluation, ablation, metric computation, or other new empirical output is required.
invocation.not_for: Not for ordinary production-code changes that create no new experiment or evaluation result.

autopilot-note.summary: Route and note artifacts, producing digests and triage proposals.

invocation.use_when: Use when existing artifacts must be collected, digested, triaged, or routed into a durable note workflow.
invocation.not_for: Not for storing one scoped memory item or for performing the primary research, code, or document work.

autopilot-refine.summary: Correct and update existing document/research artifacts while preserving snapshots and change history.

invocation.use_when: Use when an existing document or research artifact needs factual, structural, stylistic, or review-driven correction with history preserved.
invocation.not_for: Not for drafting a new artifact or performing new empirical work required before the document can change.

autopilot-research.summary: Shared upfront research that surveys academic, technology, or market sources before downstream routing.

invocation.use_when: Use when a task needs a durable survey of new academic, technology, or market evidence before downstream specification or production.
invocation.not_for: Not for repository-only project analysis, a simple factual lookup, or work already grounded by sufficient current evidence.

autopilot-ship.summary: Prepare application deployment/release setup and a ship checklist.

invocation.use_when: Use when an application needs deployment or release configuration, readiness verification, and a ship checklist.
invocation.not_for: Not for ordinary feature implementation or an unauthorized production deployment.

autopilot-spec.summary: Create or update requirements/blueprints while keeping `prd.md` as the only spec-change path.

invocation.use_when: Use when product requirements, architecture, evaluation policy, or another blueprint must be created or materially updated before implementation.
invocation.not_for: Not for implementing an already-approved specification or for editing unrelated documents.

code-execute.summary: Execute a plan step by step, delegate implementation to the development role, and record an execution log.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-code dispatches the implementation stage for an approved plan.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

code-plan.summary: Analyze code, write a detailed implementation plan, and run the plan-check gate at the rigor derived from intensity.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-code dispatches the planning and plan-check stage.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

code-refine.summary: Revise an existing plan using user notes, plan-check feedback, and verification-failure notes.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-code dispatches plan revision after user notes, plan-check feedback, or verification failure.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

code-report.summary: Assemble code-cycle results into a user-facing report.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-code dispatches the final code-cycle reporting stage.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

code-test.summary: Verify implementation results in stages and record evidence.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-code dispatches implementation verification and evidence recording.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

design-components.summary: Build UI components/mockups and preview artifacts.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-design dispatches component, mockup, or preview construction.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

design-handoff.summary: Package design results as assets and specifications for development handoff.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-design dispatches the development-handoff packaging stage.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

design-init.summary: Bootstrap the design environment and state.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-design dispatches design environment and state initialization.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

design-refs.summary: Collect external and user-provided visual references and create a brief.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-design dispatches visual-reference collection and brief creation.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

design-review.summary: Review design output for quality, token-contract compliance, and breakage.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-design dispatches design quality, token-contract, and breakage review.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

design-tokens.summary: Define design tokens such as color, typography, and spacing.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-design dispatches design-token definition or revision.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

draft-refine.summary: Refine a draft by applying memo/review feedback to a document strategy or draft.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-draft or autopilot-refine dispatches an internal strategy or draft refinement stage.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

draft-strategy.summary: Create an initial document strategy and evidence-based writing plan.

invocation.use_when: Use only when autopilot-draft dispatches document strategy and evidence-plan creation.
invocation.not_for: Not for top-level user requests or primary capability routing.

post-it.summary: Store project/cross-project notes and handoffs in working memory.

invocation.use_when: Use when the acting agent needs to store, retrieve, resolve, hand off, or promote a scoped working-memory item in support of current work.
invocation.not_for: Not for primary task routing, broad artifact triage, or replacing the capability that owns the current work.

post-it.argument_shape: [show] | add <category> <text> | resolve <hint> | decide <text> | handoff [--no-confirm] | sweep [--no-confirm] | promote [<hint>] [--scope project|user [<aspect>]]

builder.summary: Analyze, specify, implement, verify, and retain project memory without the document/design specialist surface.

full.summary: Expose every built-in capability and portable role.

starter.summary: Run the golden code task with the code pipeline and mandatory kernel guards.
